summary: Joel Spolsky announces version 3.0 of his influential 'Guerrilla Guide to Interviewing,' which originated as an internal Fog Creek hiring guide in 2000. The post serves as a brief announcement linking to the full updated guide. He notes that version 2.0 appeared in his book, and this latest rewrite reflects industry changes and new interviewing lessons learned over the years. The post opens with a humorous, absurd hypothetical scenario that appears to be a sample interview question.
key_points: The Guerrilla Guide to Interviewing has gone through three major versions since 2000, reflecting Joel's evolving thinking on hiring
The guide originated as an internal Fog Creek document before becoming one of the most widely-read resources on tech interviewing
Version 3.0 incorporates both industry changes and new personal lessons about interviewing
money_quotes: This 3.0 version is another major rewrite which reflects some of the changes in the industry over the last few years, and incorporates some new things I've learned about interviewing.

A motley gang of anarchists, free-love advocates, and banana-rights agitators have hijacked The Love Boat out of Puerto Vallarta and are threatening to sink it in 7 days with all 616 passengers and 327 crew members unless their demands are met.
key_insight: Interviewing practices need regular revision as the industry evolves, and even well-established hiring guides benefit from periodic major rewrites based on accumulated experience.
